message:
fix(pages/analytics): BTreeMap for deterministic statistics/timeline JSON [MEDIUM]

R3 lens J review finding. `AnalyticsBundle::write_to_dir` serializes
three HashMap-backed fields to JSON files via
`serde_json::to_string_pretty`:

  Statistics.agents: HashMap<String, AgentStats>      → statistics.json
  Statistics.roles:  HashMap<String, usize>           → statistics.json
  Timeline.by_agent: HashMap<String, AgentTimeline>   → timeline.json

With `preserve_order` enabled transitively in the dep tree, serde_json
serializes HashMap in iteration order — which is randomized per-run by
std's default hasher. The user-visible consequence:

  - statistics.json and timeline.json are BYTE-NON-DETERMINISTIC.
  - Running `cass` analytics regeneration twice with identical data
    produces two different files, creating diffs on every rerun,
    breaking reproducible builds, polluting git history, and
    defeating any content-hashing / incremental-backup discipline.

Same class of bug as the introspect.response_schemas non-determinism
(coding_agent_session_search-8sl73) that was fixed by swapping
HashMap → BTreeMap in src/lib.rs. This commit applies the same fix
to pages/analytics.rs:

  Statistics.agents   → BTreeMap<String, AgentStats>
  Statistics.roles    → BTreeMap<String, usize>
  Timeline.by_agent   → BTreeMap<String, AgentTimeline>

The type change is drop-in: the only HashMap APIs these fields use are
`insert()` and `get_mut()`, both identical in BTreeMap. Internal
hashmaps (`messages_by_agent`, `workspaces_by_agent`) used only during
computation stay HashMap — they don't get serialized.

Verified: cargo check + 298 analytics tests pass; test_bundle_write
exercises the JSON write path.

Severity: MEDIUM (reproducibility / hygiene; no data loss, no user
correctness break — just non-reproducible JSON files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
